behaviors.description: A request whose signature does not match its body is rejected with a 401 response; the body is not forwarded.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is handler-to-queue handoff plus HTTP mapping, not just hash math. MOCK-MISS: a pure unit test of HMAC compare would miss accidental enqueue on failure. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP are not required. Rejected higher level: behavior text only requires 401 + no forward, not real network/OS effects.
behaviors.test_description: Run the webhook route in-process with real signature verification wiring and a spy/fake queue module. Send a request with invalid `X-Signature-256`; assert HTTP 401 and assert queue `enqueue` was never called.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test requires module wiring (HTTP handler + verifier + queue boundary); unit-only would be too low.

behaviors.description: An event is written to the persistent queue before the HTTP 200 acknowledgment is returned to the sender.
behaviors.justification: This is an ordering contract across modules (handler vs persistent queue write). MOCK-MISS: unit tests on either module alone cannot prove ack-after-write ordering. REAL-INFRA not inherently required by behavior text; it does not demand real network or subprocess. Rejected higher level: no explicit OS/network/subprocess dependency in wording.
behaviors.test_description: Use in-process HTTP test harness with real handler and queue implementation (or contract-faithful test double that exposes write completion). Make queue write intentionally async; assert response is sent only after write completion signal.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Ordering across components is the core; integration is the minimum boundary.

behaviors.description: The handler returns the HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing of the event to finish.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is decoupling between request path and downstream processor completion. Needs handler + queue/processor wiring to catch real wait-on-processor bugs. REAL-INFRA not required; can reproduce with controlled in-process slow processor. Rejected higher level: no requirement for real OS/network timing beyond basic async semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Start route with downstream processor stub that blocks (e.g., unresolved promise) after enqueue. Send valid request and assert HTTP success returns promptly while processor remains unfinished.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration needed because bug appears in orchestration, not single-function logic.

behaviors.description: The response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock time even when downstream processing of earlier events is slow and concurrent.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA is explicit: wall-clock SLA under concurrency. MOCK-HIDE: fake timers/in-process stubs can miss real event-loop contention, socket scheduling, and runtime backpressure effects. No LLM dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Run the actual service process, fire concurrent webhook requests while earlier events are processed slowly, and measure real end-to-end response latency at HTTP client level. Assert each response completes <3s wall-clock.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Lower levels would not reliably expose runtime contention that violates the SLA.

behaviors.description: An event whose ID was already received is not written to the queue a second time.
behaviors.justification: Dedup correctness depends on handler + persistence/idempotency boundary. Unit test of dedup helper is insufficient for real duplicate-write failures. REAL-INFRA may not be required if local persistent store is in-process. Rejected higher level: behavior text does not require real network/OS/subprocess.
behaviors.test_description: With real dedup storage path enabled, submit event ID once then again; assert first is persisted/enqueued and second is rejected/skipped with no second queue write.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration covers cross-module state handoff where duplicates can leak.

behaviors.description: When the queue reports it is full, the handler returns 503 with a Retry-After header.
behaviors.justification: This verifies mapping of queue-capacity signal to HTTP response semantics. MOCK-MISS: unit test of status-code helper won’t catch handler/queue integration errors. REAL-INFRA not required because failure can be triggered by in-process queue-full signal. Rejected higher level: wording is about response mapping, not physical capacity enforcement under OS/network load.
behaviors.test_description: Configure queue interface to return a `full` condition on enqueue attempt. Send valid webhook; assert HTTP 503 and presence/value format of `Retry-After` header.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration is minimum to validate cross-module error propagation into HTTP behavior.

behaviors.description: The persistent queue actually enforces its configured capacity limit and rejects events that would exceed it.
behaviors.justification: This targets queue implementation behavior with persistence/config interaction. Needs more than unit logic because stateful storage boundaries are involved. Under LEVEL-DEFS, local DB/filesystem persistence fits Integration. Rejected higher level: behavior does not explicitly require real network/subprocess; if backend is local persistence, System is not mandatory.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate queue with small configured capacity on its real persistence backend used in tests (e.g., local DB/filesystem). Fill to limit, attempt one more enqueue, assert deterministic rejection and no persisted overflow record.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test description uses real queue+persistence module interaction, matching Integration floor.
